description: Add workspace dependencies: clap-verbosity-flag, tracing-appender, update tracing-subscriber features
steps: Add tracing-appender = "0.2" to [workspace.dependencies] in Cargo.toml
Add clap-verbosity-flag = { version = "3", default-features = false, features = ["tracing"] } to [workspace.dependencies] in Cargo.toml
Update tracing-subscriber in [workspace.dependencies] to { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "json"] }
Add clap-verbosity-flag = { workspace = true } to crates/aipm/Cargo.toml [dependencies]
Add tracing-subscriber = { workspace = true } to crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml [dependencies]
Add tracing-appender = { workspace = true } to crates/libaipm/Cargo.toml [dependencies]
Run cargo build --workspace to verify all dependencies resolve correctly
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint issues

description: Create libaipm::logging module with LogFormat enum and init() subscriber function
steps: Create new file crates/libaipm/src/logging.rs
Define pub enum LogFormat { Text, Json }
Implement pub fn init(verbosity: LevelFilter, format: LogFormat) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>
Create file appender layer: RollingFileAppender with DAILY rotation, 'aipm' prefix, 'log' suffix, max 7 files, targeting std::env::temp_dir()
Create file layer: fmt::layer() with .with_writer(file_appender), .with_ansi(false), .with_target(true), .with_filter(LevelFilter::DEBUG)
Implement AIPM_LOG env var check: if std::env::var("AIPM_LOG").is_ok() use EnvFilter::from_env("AIPM_LOG"), else use EnvFilter from verbosity level
Implement Text format branch: fmt::layer().with_writer(std::io::stderr).with_target(true).with_filter(stderr_filter)
Implement Json format branch: fmt::layer().json().with_writer(std::io::stderr).with_target(true).with_file(true).with_line_number(true).with_filter(stderr_filter)
Wire both stderr and file layers into tracing_subscriber::registry().with(...).with(...).init()
Add pub mod logging; to crates/libaipm/src/lib.rs
Run cargo build --workspace to verify compilation
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint issues
Ensure no use of .unwrap(), .expect(), panic!(), println!(), or any denied lint patterns

description: Add -v/-q verbosity flags and --log-format flag to aipm Cli struct via clap-verbosity-flag
steps: Add use clap_verbosity_flag::{Verbosity, WarnLevel}; to crates/aipm/src/main.rs
Add #[command(flatten)] verbose: Verbosity<WarnLevel> field to Cli struct
Add #[arg(long, default_value = "text", value_parser = ["text", "json"])] log_format: String field to Cli struct
Verify the flag mapping: -qq=OFF, -q=ERROR, none=WARN, -v=INFO, -vv=DEBUG, -vvv=TRACE
Run cargo build --workspace to verify Cli struct compiles
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Wire logging::init() into aipm run() function to activate tracing subscriber on startup
steps: In crates/aipm/src/main.rs run() function, after Cli::parse(), extract verbosity: let verbosity = cli.verbose.tracing_level_filter();
Parse log_format: match cli.log_format.as_str() to convert to libaipm::logging::LogFormat enum
Call libaipm::logging::init(verbosity, format)? before the match on cli.command
Verify existing tracing::info! call at main.rs:327 (workspace root found) now produces output with -v flag
Run cargo build --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all existing tests still pass
Refactor resolve_plugins_dir to use match on manifest::load() result instead of if let Ok
Add tracing::debug!(path = %manifest_path.display(), "manifest has no plugins_dir, using .ai") for the no-plugins_dir case
Add tracing::debug!(path = %manifest_path.display(), error = %e, "could not load manifest, using .ai") for the Err case
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace
Identify where Config::default() is used as fallback when manifest parsing fails
Add tracing::warn! or tracing::debug! call logging the parse failure and fallback to default config
Include the manifest path and error details in structured tracing fields
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace
Locate where unparseable manifests are silently skipped during workspace root walk
Add tracing::debug! call logging the skipped manifest path and parse error
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace
Locate where packages with unparseable versions are silently skipped
Add tracing::warn! call logging the package name and version parse error
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace
Locate where invalid glob patterns are silently skipped
Add tracing::warn!(pattern = %pat, error = %e, "ignoring invalid glob pattern in ignore list")
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Instrument silent fallbacks in scan functions (lint/rules/scan.rs:32-131)
steps: Locate scan_skills, scan_agents, scan_hook_files functions
Add tracing::debug! calls where unreadable directories or files are silently skipped
Include the path and error in structured tracing fields
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Instrument silent fallback in store link_to (store/mod.rs:160-186) — verify existing tracing::warn! is adequate
steps: Read store/mod.rs:160-186 to check existing tracing::warn! call for hard-link to copy fallback
Verify the existing tracing::warn! has sufficient structured fields (source, dest)
Add or improve structured fields if needed
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Instrument silent fallback in copilot_extension_detector try_read_config (migrate/copilot_extension_detector.rs:60-79)
steps: Locate where 9 candidate config filenames are tried silently
Add tracing::debug! calls logging each attempted filename and whether it was found or skipped
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Instrument silent fallbacks in migrate emitter and cleanup (emitter.rs:618-626, cleanup.rs:64-71)
steps: In migrate/emitter.rs:618-626, add tracing::warn! for files that fail to read during migration
In migrate/cleanup.rs:64-71, add tracing::warn! for directories that fail to read during pruning
Include file/directory path and error in structured tracing fields
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Instrument silent fallback in fs.rs atomic write backup cleanup (fs.rs:201-205)
steps: Locate where atomic write backup cleanup silently discards errors on Windows
Add tracing::debug! call logging the cleanup failure path and error
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace
Create test module in crates/libaipm/src/logging.rs
Test init() with LevelFilter::WARN and LogFormat::Text does not error
Test init() with LevelFilter::DEBUG and LogFormat::Json does not error
Test init() with LevelFilter::OFF and LogFormat::Text does not error
Test that LogFormat enum variants are correctly distinguishable
Note: tracing global subscriber can only be set once per process; use tracing::subscriber::with_default for test isolation or test in separate integration tests
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Integration tests for CLI verbosity flags and log format
steps: Add integration test: aipm --help still works (smoke test)
Add integration test: aipm with no flags produces no tracing output on stderr for clean operations
Add integration test: aipm -v produces INFO-level output on stderr
Add integration test: aipm -q suppresses warnings on stderr
Add integration test: aipm --log-format=json produces valid JSON on stderr when combined with -v
Add integration test: AIPM_LOG=debug overrides default warn level
Add integration test: verify file log is created in temp dir after operation
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: Coverage verification — ensure all new code meets 89% branch coverage gate
steps: Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov clean --workspace
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --workspace --branch
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov --no-report --doc
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov report --doctests --branch --ignore-filename-regex '(tests/|research/|specs/|wizard_tty\.rs)'
Verify TOTAL line branch column shows >= 89%
If coverage is below threshold, add additional tests for uncovered branches
Run cargo fmt --check to verify formatting
